intent: Add a 'View run' affordance on agent comments whose source_task_id resolves to a task. U1 stamps source_task_id on synthesized-success comment (task.go); U2 backfills historical via migration 158 with workspace guard and DISTINCT ON for 1:N; U3+U4 add the sync cache hook (useAgentTaskForComment) + ViewRunButton chip + GC hover-card explainer; plus Go + Vitest tests and a rollout runbook.

findings.title: Migration 118 lint stems mismatch (P0 from data-migration; duplicate echoed by correctness/adversarial)
findings.fix_summary: Removed '118' from legacyDuplicateMigrationStems (118 is single-stem on disk and already in isKnownLegacyPrefix). Kept '119' (legitimately a duplicate — two real 119 files share the prefix). Verified with `go test -v -run TestMigration ./internal/migrations/...`.
findings.first_evidence: server/internal/migrations/migrations_lint_test.go:43 (pre-fix): `"118": {"118_workspace_invitation_preauth", "118_workspace_invitation_welcome_seen"}` — both stems absent on disk; `ls server/migrations/ | grep ^118` returns only 118_agent_task_queue_initiator_user_id_drop_fk
findings.why_it_matters: Pre-existing CI red — `TestMigrationNumericPrefixesStayUniqueAfterLegacySet` would fail because `reflect.DeepEqual` of actual vs. expected stem lists does not match. Blocks migration 158 from being deployed.

findings.title: useSyncExternalStore subscribes to entire query cache, over-triggering re-renders
findings.fix_summary: Filtered the subscriber by `event.query.queryKey` — only fires on mutations to ['issues','tasks', issueId] (same invalidation scope the WS task:* aggregator uses).
findings.first_evidence: packages/views/issues/hooks/use-agent-task-for-comment.ts:36 (pre-fix): `const subscribe = (onChange) => queryClient.getQueryCache().subscribe(() => onChange())` — fires on every cache mutation app-wide
findings.why_it_matters: On a long issue with many agent comments and frequent task:* invalidations, every comment card would re-evaluate on every cache mutation, defeating the memoization contract (R10/AE6) and degrading scroll performance.

findings.title: ViewRunButton accepts AgentTask without workspace re-validation; corrupted source_task_id bypasses cache guard
findings.fix_summary: Added `t.issue_id === issueId` guard inside getSnapshot so a task from a different workspace/issue returns null. ViewRunButtonSlot already passes issueId through to the hook, so the guard applies at the resolver layer.
findings.first_evidence: packages/views/issues/hooks/use-agent-task-for-comment.ts:39 (pre-fix): `return tasks.find((t) => t.id === sourceTaskId) ?? null` — identity check only, no workspace scope
findings.why_it_matters: Without the guard, a corrupted source_task_id (e.g. from a failed migration or data import) referencing a task in a different workspace would open the wrong-workspace transcript silently (cache hit; no GC explainer).

findings.title: Suppression race: View run button and GC explainer fire simultaneously between WS event and 750ms cache invalidation
findings.first_evidence: packages/views/issues/components/comment-card.tsx:393: `<ViewRunButtonSlot ... />` is followed by `<GcTooltipSlot ... />`; both read useAgentTaskForComment in the same render pass
findings.why_it_matters: After a task:completed WS event, the aggregateRefreshTimer (750ms debounce) clears the issue-tasks cache. During that window, source_task_id is set on the comment (U1 stamps it going-forward) but the AgentTask is not in the cache, so both ViewRunButtonSlot (renders null) and GcTooltipSlot (renders the explainer) compete. Acceptable at current scale; self-healing, but user-visible as a 750ms explainer flash on every successful run.

findings.title: Cascade failure: migration 158 partial completion + U1 rollback leaves backfilled rows in inconsistent state
findings.first_evidence: server/migrations/158_backfill_comment_source_task_id.up.sql:44: `WHERE c.source_task_id IS NULL` guard prevents double-fill but provides no progress tracking; documented rollback SQL undoes ALL backfilled rows (not just cross-workspace ones)
findings.why_it_matters: If the migration crashes at 80% and the operator rolls back U1, going-forward writes revert to NULL. The only soft-rollback path (per the runbook) clears ALL backfilled rows, conflating two distinct remediation scenarios. Defer for design call: should the soft-rollback distinguish 'rows that were correct' from 'rows that were wrong'?

findings.title: Stale cache after soft navigation: View run button silently missing with no loading state
findings.first_evidence: packages/views/issues/hooks/use-agent-task-for-comment.ts:44: `useSyncExternalStore` exposes no loading state; on soft navigation where the cache was evicted, both ViewRunButtonSlot and GcTooltipSlot render null (sourceTaskId null OR not yet resolved)
findings.why_it_matters: Defer for design call: should the chip show a loading skeleton until cache resolves, or silently no-render? Today it silently no-renders, which is the same surface as 'task was GC'd' — users cannot distinguish. Out of scope for MVP but worth tracking.

findings.title: Rapid re-trigger abuse: sequential agent re-triggers produce duplicate GC explainers on sequential comments
findings.first_evidence: packages/views/issues/components/comment-card.tsx:439: GcTooltipSlot renders one explainer per comment; when an agent is rapidly re-triggered, multiple comments get the same explanation visible in the thread
findings.why_it_matters: Acceptable noise at current scale; documented as a design tradeoff (one explainer per clean-up, not per task). Defer.

findings.title: Empty-hover-card content guard: GcTooltipSlot renders HoverCard even when i18n message resolves to empty string
findings.first_evidence: packages/views/issues/components/comment-card.tsx:439: `<HoverCardTrigger ... aria-label={message} />` — HoverCard opens on hover regardless of message content
findings.why_it_matters: Low likelihood (i18n keys are static in the four locale files; no path produces an empty message today). A one-line defensive check `if (!message) return null;` is the fix. Defer for trivial cleanup.

findings.title: Backend test deferred cleanup panics when authRequest throws before response is assigned
findings.first_evidence: server/cmd/server/task_source_task_id_test.go:908: `resp.Body.Close()` in t.Cleanup; resp is not checked for nil before close
findings.why_it_matters: If authRequest throws, resp is nil and the cleanup panics, hiding the original error. Same pattern at line 946. A two-character nil check is the fix; defer for trivial cleanup.

findings.title: ViewRunButton is a thin wrapper with no added logic
findings.first_evidence: packages/views/issues/components/view-run-button.tsx: 39 lines, forwards all four props verbatim to TranscriptButton; ViewRunButtonSlot in comment-card.tsx serves as the semantic seam
findings.why_it_matters: Deleting ViewRunButton and inlining `<TranscriptButton ... />` in the two call-sites would reduce a file and an indirection hop. Low priority: the i18n hook is the only thing the wrapper adds.

findings.title: Go test file advertises 5 cases but contains 2 — 3 suppression branches deferred
findings.first_evidence: server/cmd/server/task_source_task_id_test.go: 2 Test* functions; the plan's U5 advertised 5 cases including 3 suppression branches (trivial-output, intermediate-agent-comment, retryable-failure)
findings.why_it_matters: The 3 suppression branches are independent of U1's actual fix (which is on the assignment-style path that never triggers the trivial-output or retryable-failure suppressions). The plan's U5 scope was over-promised. Out of scope for this run; track as deferred follow-up.

findings.title: Vitest file advertises 5 cases but only 4 are real after the new test count; 1 GC/stale-cache path deferred
findings.first_evidence: packages/views/issues/hooks/use-agent-task-for-commit.test.ts: 6 cases (null sourceTaskId, unpopulated cache, matching task, cross-issue, non-matching id, memo stability); the testing reviewer count of 5 predates the new cross-issue test added during this review
findings.why_it_matters: The review-time count was off-by-one (tester was reading the diff before the round-2 review added the cross-issue case). Current state: 6 cases passing. Out of scope; the GC/subscription path was deliberately excluded — see #6 (stale cache) for the design call.

findings.title: Trailing newlines: only task_source_task_id_test.go was missing a final newline
findings.fix_summary: Appended a single newline to task_source_task_id_test.go. Other flagged files (view-run-button.tsx, use-agent-task-for-comment.ts, 158.up.sql) already terminated with a newline.
findings.first_evidence: view-run-button.tsx:572 (pre-fix): file ends with bytes 0x7d 0x29 0x3b without a final 0x0a newline (verified via xxd)
findings.why_it_matters: Cosmetic; fixes a single byte difference that inconsistent file terminations can cause in tooling chains.

findings.title: Migration 119 lint stems confirmed correct — both stems exist on disk
findings.first_evidence: server/internal/migrations/migrations_lint_test.go:44: `"119": {"119_invitation_invitee_name", "119_user_created_at_index"}` — both stems present on disk (verified via ls)
findings.why_it_matters: Resolution of the round-1 P0 fix: 119 is legitimately a duplicate and the legacy-duplicate-stems entry is correct. Note in the round-2 review log: the adversarial reviewer's P1 finding claimed 118/119 were equally self-conflicting, but verification showed 119 is real-duplicate and 118 was not — the fix kept 119, removed 118 only.

findings.title: Migration lint test modified without plan coverage (scope observation)
findings.first_evidence: server/internal/migrations/migrations_lint_test.go:45: modification of the lint test was unplanned in the U2 plan; required to make migration 158 deployable
findings.why_it_matters: The lint test addition was a pre-existing CI red that blocked migration 158's deployment. Modifying the lint test was necessary but not in plan scope; consider retroactively documenting the lint-test fix in the plan or as a ce-compound learning.

actionable_findings.title: Suppression race between View run button and GC explainer (P2)
actionable_findings.first_evidence: packages/views/issues/components/comment-card.tsx:393 — ViewRunButtonSlot and GcTooltipSlot both read useAgentTaskForComment
actionable_findings.suggested_fix: Decide: should one of the two slots be guarded by an `isResolving` flag, or accept the 750ms flash as inherent to the WS debounce? Add an acceptance test for the flash window if accepting.

actionable_findings.title: Cascade: partial migration + U1 rollback recovery path (P1)
actionable_findings.first_evidence: server/migrations/158_backfill_comment_source_task_id.up.sql:44 — no progress tracking in UPDATE; soft-rollback clears ALL backfilled rows
actionable_findings.suggested_fix: Two design choices: (a) add a progress table to the migration (e.g. migration_progress(backfilled_count)) updated in a trigger; (b) refine the soft-rollback to only NULL rows that fall in the cross-workspace mismatch category. Recommend (b) — less intrusive.

actionable_findings.title: Stale cache loading state (P2)
actionable_findings.suggested_fix: Decide: skeleton on cache miss, or silent no-render (current). Update rollout runbook if silent.

actionable_findings.title: Rapid re-trigger duplicate GC explainers (P2)
actionable_findings.first_evidence: packages/views/issues/components/comment-card.tsx:439 — one explainer per comment, not per task

actionable_findings.title: Empty-hover-card content guard (P3)
actionable_findings.first_evidence: packages/views/issues/components/comment-card.tsx:439 — GcTooltipSlot renders HoverCard even with empty message
actionable_findings.suggested_fix: Add `if (!message) return null;` to GcTooltipSlot before the HoverCard. Two-character fix.

actionable_findings.title: Backend test nil check in t.Cleanup (P3)
actionable_findings.suggested_fix: Wrap with `if resp != nil { resp.Body.Close() }` in both t.Cleanup blocks.

actionable_findings.title: ViewRunButton thin wrapper (P3 advisory)
actionable_findings.suggested_fix: Delete the wrapper and inline `<TranscriptButton>` at the two call-sites in comment-card.tsx. The i18n hook moves into the call-sites.

actionable_findings.title: Go test 5→2 cases mismatch (P1 advisory)
actionable_findings.suggested_fix: Add 3 suppression-branch tests (trivial-output, intermediate-agent-comment, retryable-failure) — out of scope for this run. The 2 current tests cover U1's actual fix (assignment-style success + failure paths).

triage_groups.title: Migration 158 rollout safety — design calls deferred for operator follow-up
triage_groups.context: P1 cascade (migration rollback ambiguity) + P2 duplicate GC explainer noise both surface after migration runs in production. Operators need decision-grade guidance; the runbook's current soft-rollback SQL conflates two scenarios.
triage_groups.preferred_resolution: Refine soft-rollback in the runbook to only NULL rows in the cross-workspace mismatch category (per the workspace-guard Query B) rather than all backfilled rows. Accept rapid-retrigger duplicate explainers as inherent noise.
triage_groups.why: Both are operator-experience issues that the rollout runbook must address, not code fixes. Resolve together so operators have a single decision point.

triage_groups.title: Hook UX edge cases — no-render vs skeleton
triage_groups.context: P2 suppression race (View run + GC explainer fire simultaneously during the 750ms WS debounce) and P2 stale-cache silent failure on soft navigation. Both are UX edge cases on the sync cache hook.
triage_groups.preferred_resolution: Decide one direction: keep silent no-render (current; matches 'no affordance = no state to manage') or add a skeleton during cache miss. The cascade's 750ms debounce and the soft-navigation cache eviction produce visually similar empty states today; aligning on a single UX prevents two slightly different states from being deployed later. Defer to a UX design call.
triage_groups.why: Both surface in the same hook at the same lifecycle (cache empty / cache stale). Resolve together to keep the hook contract consistent.

triage_groups.title: Trivial cleanup — defer to follow-up PR
triage_groups.context: Three trivial fixes (empty-hover-card guard, Go test nil check, ViewRunButton wrapper removal) plus the Go test 5→2 scope observation. None block merge; all are 1-3 line changes.
triage_groups.preferred_resolution: Open a follow-up PR after merge that bundles (8) + (9) + (10) + (11). The test scope observation (11) is informational only — the 2 current tests cover the actual U1 fix; the deferred branches were plan over-promises.
triage_groups.why: Group small cleanups for one review pass; do not pad the MVP commit with stylistic noise.

requirements_completeness.summary: Plan execution complete against the 12 R-IDs. 6 of 6 implementation units landed: U1 (source_task_id stamp) + U2 (migration 158) + U3 (View run chip) + U4 (GC hover-card) + U5 (focused tests: Go DB-backed + Vitest hook; e2e + comment-card integration deferred) + U6 (rollout runbook). Outstanding Questions deferred to implementation: workspace guard SQL root (resolved via JOIN issue guard), tooltip trigger area (resolved as (c) dedicated hit-area), R10 memo strategy (useSyncExternalStore is the path).

rows.note: X-Task-ID handler path at handler/comment.go:1234-1262 was confirmed correct; not modified.
Retracted from requirements during planning; documented in scope boundaries as 'not a task run product'.
Cross-issue workspace guard added during review (P2 reliability fix).
Trigger area selected: (c) dedicated hit-area (HoverCard with H4-sized hit area). UX closure deferred to implementation as planned.
Memo stability enforced by useSyncExternalStore (referential identity preserved for unchanged cache state) + scope-filtered subscription (review fix).

learnings.name: Affordance reuse in comment context
learnings.relevance: validates KTD1: the ViewRunButton must wrap TranscriptButton, not build a parallel chip. Affordances depending on run-domain data in comment context must import and wrap existing rich components, never build simplified duplicates. The packages/ui vs packages/views boundary makes the cheaper path irreversible.

learnings.name: Forward-only backfill migration
learnings.relevance: validates migration 158's down.sql = SELECT 1 pattern. One-way backfills are documented as no-op from the start; rollback is only the manual NULL-out SQL when query B shows cross-workspace leakage. The runbook's monitoring queries A-D operationalize this pattern.

learnings.name: Cache as truth boundary, not re-validation point
learnings.relevance: validates useAgentTaskForComment's deliberate choice NOT to re-validate the AgentTask object at the hook layer. useSyncExternalStore requires snapshot identity preservation; any re-validation at the hook layer would produce a new reference on every render, breaking memo stability for zero security gain. Trust parseWithFallback at the API boundary (the cache write site) and the workspace-scoped cache key for cross-issue defense.

learnings.name: useSyncExternalStore sync-cache-only hook pattern
learnings.relevance: The combination of (a) useSyncExternalStore subscribed to a specific queryKey, (b) getQueryData synchronous read, (c) no useQuery, (d) no event subscription beyond the query cache, (e) workspace-guard inside getSnapshot — is a reusable pattern for any 'show a UI affordance tied to a specific cache key, with a workspace boundary' use case. Worth promoting to docs/solutions/design-patterns/.

learnings.name: Dedicated hit-area decision over card-wide hover
learnings.relevance: GcTooltipSlot's choice of a dedicated small hit-area for hover-card explainers (decision (c) of the R9 deferred decision) — avoids accidental triggers during text selection and scrolling, gives a predictable affordance. A reusable pattern for any 'explanation-on-miss' UI element that should not hijack card-wide interactions.

agent_native_gaps.name: no_action_required
agent_native_gaps.summary: Multica has two agent integration surfaces: the daemon runtime (long-running process authenticating as an agent) and the Composio MCP overlay. The 'View run' chip is purely a user-facing UI affordance over the existing GET /api/tasks/:id/messages endpoint — it does not introduce a new agent capability, tool, or API. The existing transcript endpoint is one of the existing daemon-facing surfaces and is unchanged. No action required.

deployment_notes: Pre-deploy (3 GO gates): staging dry-run with BEGIN/ROLLBACK transaction; baseline SQL capture (eligible_total + already_stamped); pre-existing cross-workspace leakage check that must return 0.
Deploy sequence: U1+U3+U4 code first, verify going-forward stamp live, then migration 158 in a low-traffic window.

Top-3 post-deploy verifications (operators' non-negotiable): (1) Query B returns 0 cross-workspace rows; (2) stamped_total delta > 0 when eligible_total was non-zero; (3) 10-row spot-check shows comment_ws = task_ws.
Rollback matrix: U1 revert safe; U3/U4 revert safe; U2 forward-only (no automated rollback) — soft-rollback via documented NULL-out UPDATE only warranted if Query B surfaces non-zero rows.
residual_risks: Migration 158 workspace guard depends on comment.workspace_id being non-NULL. Pre-migration-025 deployments with NULL workspace_id silently skip backfill. Pre-deployment audit of NULL workspace_id comment rows is required.

GcTooltipSlot renders transiently during normal use when the sidebar cache hasn't been populated yet for a newly opened issue — users see the 'cleaned up' tooltip until the WS debounce fires (max 750ms). Self-healing, user-visible as a tooltip flash.

Plan assumption that ExecutionLogSection renders before CommentCard is not enforced in code. A future reordering that makes the sidebar lazy-loaded would break both the View run button and the GC tooltip silently — ViewRunButtonSlot would render null because the cache hasn't been populated.

Migration 158 DISTINCT ON picks the earliest task when multiple tasks share a trigger_comment_id. The comment links to the earlier task even when a later retry is more meaningful. Accepted by design but operators should be aware.
testing_gaps: No test for entry.source_task_id = '' (empty string). The null-coalescing converts it to null for ViewRunButtonSlot (button hidden, correct) but GcTooltipSlot sees !'' = true and would show tooltip. Correct by coincidence, not by contract.
No test for useSyncExternalStore server/client snapshot divergence path.
No SQL-level migration unit test for the workspace guard (most safety-critical part of migration 158). The lint test is a static check, not a semantic one.
No e2e tests (e2e/issues/run-view-button.spec.ts, run-view-button-backfill.spec.ts) — require a running stack and were deferred to the operator's follow-up per U5's plan scope.
No test for concurrent safety of migration 158 — two simultaneous sessions should produce correct non-overlapping results without deadlocks.
The plan's U5 advertised 5 Go test cases; only 2 are present (the 3 suppression branches were deferred). Documented in the runbook's testing_gaps section.

coverage.cross_model_skip_reason: no Codex peer CLI on this host
coverage.stage_5b_validator_wave: skipped (mode:agent is report-only; caller is ce-work and applies review-driven fixes)
outstanding_pre_existing_issues: 118 migration lint stems mismatch: fixed in dcef1323f (P0)
Trailing newline on task_source_task_id_test.go: fixed in dcef1323f (P3)
coverage.intake_notes: all 11 reviewer personas returned. in-process review only (no cross-model peer). All fixes applied during review were committed before this report (commits dcef1323f, 006a900af). typecheck + 1798/1798 vitest passing at report time.
